label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out the best title for this passage about body language in the office. The passage starts by talking about how body language is powerful and that people often communicate more through their body movements than words. Then it lists several things you shouldn't do in the office with body language: don't touch coworkers, don't avoid eye contact, don't have a bad handshake, and don't lean your head while listening. Looking at the options: A) Pay attention to your body language at the office. This seems to fit because the whole passage is about being careful with your body language in the office setting. B) How to get along with your co-workers. While the advice might help in getting along, the main focus is specifically on body language, not general coworker relations. C) The meaning of body language you should know. This is broader and more about understanding
